summary.scholar: On April 19, 1995, a truck bomb detonated by domestic terrorist Timothy McVeigh destroyed the Alfred P. Murrah Federal Building in Oklahoma City, killing 168 people including 19 children in a daycare center. It was the deadliest act of domestic terrorism in American history until September 11, 2001. McVeigh was executed in 2001.
summary.explorer: On April 19, 1995, a terrible bombing destroyed a government building in Oklahoma City, killing 168 people — including 19 children who were in a daycare center inside. An American named Timothy McVeigh did it because he was angry at the government. It was the worst domestic terrorist attack until 9/11.

significance.scholar: The Oklahoma City bombing demonstrated the threat of domestic terrorism and led to significant changes in federal building security and anti-terrorism legislation.
significance.explorer: This attack showed that terrorism could come from inside America too, not just from foreign countries.

sources.citation: Miller Center: U.S. Presidents and Key Events
